zotero.abstractNote: Object detectors are typically evaluated using aggregate metrics like mean Average Precision (mAP). However, these metrics fail to provide insights into the specific conditions under which a model fails, which is critical for safety-critical applications. We propose SCROD (Systematic Characterization of Robustness in Object Detection), a pipeline that identifies systematic errors in object detectors. SCROD automatically discovers clusters of images where a model consistently underperforms, characterizes these clusters by identifying common visual attributes, and provides a human-interpretable description of the failure modes. We demonstrate the effectiveness of SCROD on several state-of-the-art object detectors and show that it uncovers meaningful failure modes that are not captured by standard evaluation metrics.
